claim: The zodiac section's anomalously low glyph entropy (3.7149 bits vs. corpus mean 3.8627 bits) is fully explained by a restricted label vocabulary: the section predominantly contains short repeated labels for month names, zodiac figures, and positional markers rather than running text. This predicts that the zodiac section's type-token ratio and word length distribution differ significantly from prose sections.
evidence_for: Zodiac entropy (3.7149 bits) is the lowest of all sections, 0.186 bits below the text-only maximum
Zodiac corpus is small (1,297 words over 12 folios), consistent with label-dominant rather than prose content
Astronomical section also has low entropy (3.7471 bits) and is similarly diagram-heavy with labels
evidence_against: Low entropy could also result from a different cipher key applied to the same underlying language rather than a restricted vocabulary
The recipes section has the largest word count (11,611) and second-highest entropy (3.8586 bits), which is consistent with prose but does not rule out alternative explanations for zodiac
test: Compute type-token ratio (TTR = unique_words / total_words) and mean word length for each section. Compute Kolmogorov-Smirnov test comparing zodiac word length distribution to herbal section word length distribution. Compute repetition rate (fraction of word tokens that are in the top-20 most frequent words) for zodiac vs. herbal.
test_metric: TTR per section; KS statistic D for zodiac vs. herbal word length distributions; repetition rate for zodiac vs. herbal
test_threshold: If zodiac TTR < 0.25 (vs. herbal TTR > 0.45) and zodiac repetition rate > 0.40 (vs. herbal < 0.20), and KS test p < 0.01, the label-vocabulary hypothesis is confirmed.

reasoning: If confirmed, this separates the zodiac section as structurally distinct from prose sections, allowing decipherment efforts to focus on prose sections where running text provides stronger statistical constraints on the underlying language.
